title: Topics | Stats NZ
content:
Statistics about people who live in New Zealand
Statistics about how efficiently production inputs (capital and labour) are used within the economy to produce output

## Gross domestic product
Statistics about New Zealand's official measure of economic growth
Statistics about people in New Zealand who are in paid employment, unemployed, and not in the labour force

## Using a combined census model for the 2023 Census
Using a combined census model for the 2023 Census will help Stats NZ produce the highest quality data possible to show us how New Zealanders are living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## Field tests for 2023 Census
Stats NZ conducts tests of our census processes and operations with the help of communities to get everything ready for the 2023 Census.

## Design of the 2023 Census
Census design explains how we will prepare for and deliver the 2023 Census, including how we are responding to what was learned from the 2018 Census.
Statistics and commentary about the effects of COVID-19 coronavirus in New Zealand.
Statistics about animal farming, crop growing, and forestry and farm practices

## Ahurea | Māori culture
Statistics about Māori culture, including tikanga Māori and knowledge of tūrangawaewae and pepeha.

## Ōhanga | Māori economic wellbeing
Economic wellbeing statistics, including work, income, and Māori business statistics.

## Tikanga tatauranga | Methods for statistics
Information about how we produce statistics for and about Māori, and work to develop new ways to produce these statistics.
